chunk_summary: Low-cost Indian meal plans for chronic undernutrition focus on age-appropriate, energy-dense meals with measurable follow-up goals to improve adherence and growth outcomes.
learning_objectives: Design low-cost, age-appropriate meal plans for children with chronic undernutrition.
Identify measurable follow-up goals to monitor adherence and growth velocity.
Incorporate PM POSHAN school meal norms into daily nutrition plans.
key_facts: For toddlers (12–23 months), meals should include thick porridge, khichdi, and continued breastfeeding where applicable.
School-age children should consume a protein-rich breakfast and daily school meals under PM POSHAN norms.
Adolescents benefit from pairing iron-rich foods with vitamin C and prioritizing a protein-rich breakfast.
Follow-up for stunting without wasting should occur every 2–4 weeks initially, then every 6–8 weeks.
For wasting with or without stunting, follow-up should be more frequent, aligned to risk.
reference_quote: Ensure daily school meal uptake where available, and prescribe an after-school snack such as roasted chana, peanuts, or a home-prepared chikki instead of packaged snacks.
